content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic ("This app needs camera access.") and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a placeholder literal "TODO" (Info.plist), while no location usage even appears in code (only camera scanning in ScannerView.swift).

Pierre: Apple requires purpose strings to clearly explain why the specific feature needs access, and a literal "TODO" will almost certainly get the app rejected at review since it's an obvious unfinished placeholder. Also, since ScannerView.swift only uses `AVCaptureDevice` for camera scanning, there's no visible feature justifying the location permission at all — either remove the location key/entitlement or implement and describe the feature it belongs to. Fix the camera string to reference the document-scanning feature specifically, e.g. "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents."
